nativeRange.habitat: Reef slopes, overhangs, and sheltered areas on tropical reefs, from well-lit upper zones to more dimly lit mid-depth environments

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae symbionts; lacks visible polyps and tentacles. Has been reported to benefit from supplemental phytoplankton additions a few times per week. Calcium, strontium, magnesium, and trace element supplementation are generally advised to support skeletal growth.

avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals with long sweeper tentacles placed in direct contact

imageCandidates.notes: Nick Hobgood, 1500x2000. Pachyseris speciosa: sharp in-situ shot of the whole plating colony showing the diagnostic concentric parallel ridges/valleys ('elephant skin/corduroy'), with a neon triplefin resting on it. Best all-around genus representative.
Ryan McMinds, large. Pachyseris speciosa: crisp full plating colony in situ, tan/brown, clear corduroy ridge pattern; second angle of the type species.
Ryan McMinds, 2964x2460. Pachyseris rugosa (Great Barrier Reef): whole colony showing the frillier, folded foliose growth form distinct from P. speciosa; adds a second species to the genus set.
Ryan McMinds, large. Pachyseris rugosa: sharp closer view of the tightly folded ridged fronds; complements the wide colony shot.
Diego Delso, 3367x3367. Pachyseris speciosa (Red Sea): tack-sharp macro of the pastel corduroy ridge surface; tight feature crop kept as an extra to showcase the diagnostic texture and colour.
Diego Delso, 5702x3801. Pachyseris speciosa (Philippines): high-res macro of the warm orange/tan parallel ridges; texture close-up extra, non-recommended.

summary: The Pachyseris Coral, also sold as Elephant Skin Coral and Corduroy Coral, is a plating SPS coral prized for its distinctive ridged surface texture. It is generally considered more tolerant of lower light than many SPS corals, making it approachable for intermediate reef keepers.
careNotes:
Note: Pachyseris is a genus with multiple species (including P. speciosa, P. rugosa, and P. inattesa) sold collectively in the aquarium trade under shared common names.

Pachyseris corals belong to the family Agariciidae and are grouped with SPS corals in the aquarium trade, though they are generally considered more tolerant of lower light than classic SPS genera like Acropora or Montipora. They form plating or cup-shaped colonies with characteristic parallel ridges and valleys that give rise to the common names 'Elephant Skin Coral' and 'Corduroy Coral.'

Key care notes:
• Lighting: Generally advised to keep at moderate PAR levels (around 100–250 PAR). Pachyseris is found on reef slopes and under overhangs in the wild, which suggests some adaptability to lower-light conditions. New specimens are best acclimated gradually, starting at lower light and adjusting upward if desired.
• Flow: Moderate water movement is generally recommended. Strong direct laminar flow may stress the tissue; alternating or randomized moderate flow tends to work well.
• Placement: Mid-level rockwork placement is typically advised, though this coral can tolerate a range of positions.
• Aggression: Pachyseris lacks visible polyps or sweeper tentacles, making it among the most peaceful of SPS corals. It is generally considered reef-safe with respect to neighbors, though standard coral-spacing practices are still advisable.
• Supplements: Calcium, alkalinity (kH), magnesium, strontium, and trace elements are generally advised to support skeletal calcification, consistent with standard SPS reef chemistry.
• Growth form: Colonies develop as encrusting plates that follow the underlying substrate contour, which can make them appear slow-growing, though they are steady growers under good conditions.
breedingNotes: Fragging is the primary propagation method in captivity. Plating colonies can be fragmented with bone cutters or a bandsaw; fragments readily encrust onto frag plugs or rubble pieces. Sexual reproduction occurs via broadcast spawning on natural reefs but is not practiced in the aquarium hobby.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided care level (Moderate), common names (Elephant Skin, Corduroy, Castle Coral), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), specific gravity (1.023–1.025), dKH (8–12), temperament (Peaceful), lighting description, flow description, and feeding notes.

additional.name: ReefBuilders – Pachyseris inattesa species description
additional.notes: Used for wild ecology supplemental data only: depth range 10–35 m and colony diameter 10–25 cm (for P. inattesa, a Red Sea congener newly described ca. 2020). Not used for husbandry parameters. This source does not confirm high-stakes fields and does not count toward the minimum-confirmation bar.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Pachyseris (taxonomy stub)
additional.notes: Confirmed family Agariciidae and order Scleractinia. Article is a stub with minimal content; described distribution as 'almost cosmopolitan.' Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply (BRS) — WWC Pinstripe Pachyseris
additional.notes: Second species-specific source resolving needs_review flag. Confirms temp 75-78°F, SG 1.024-1.026, pH 8.1-8.4, kH 8-9, Ca 380-450, Mg 1300-1450, PAR 150-250 medium, moderate flow, mid-level rockwork placement, moderate care level, peaceful aggression. Photosynthesis-primary feeding; amino acid supplements may enhance growth and coloration.
